channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Advice on going back to work after parental leave
item.description: Heading back to work after parental leave? Life Kit has some advice on navigating schedules, managing expectations and finding support during a major life transition.
item.content:encoded: Heading back to work after parental leave? Life Kit has some advice on navigating schedules, managing expectations and finding support during a major life transition.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: Why this moment matters so much to New York Knicks fans
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Trent Simonian, creator of the SideTalk social media account, about Knicks fandom as they approach Game 5 of the NBA Finals, which, if they win, would make them champs.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Trent Simonian, creator of the SideTalk social media account, about Knicks fandom as they approach Game 5 of the NBA Finals, which, if they win, would make them champs.
item.dc:creator: Ailsa Chang

item.title: The Teddy Bear Doctor treats stuffies in need of care
item.description: Ruth Hasman's home in British Columbia is known as the teddy bear hospital -- a place where stuffies in need of repair receive the TLC they need.
item.content:encoded: Ruth Hasman's home in British Columbia is known as the teddy bear hospital -- a place where stuffies in need of repair receive the TLC they need.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: How companies can sneak new chemicals past the FDA — and into our food and supplements
item.description: There's a sneaky way companies add new chemicals in our food, and it is there by design, and totally legal.
item.content:encoded: There's a sneaky way companies add new chemicals in our food, and it is there by design, and totally legal.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Gonzalez

item.title: LA mayoral candidate says she's frustrated with current mayor's 'lack of urgency'
item.description: The votes have been counted, and the mayoral incumbent in Los Angeles -- Karen Bass -- will face fellow Democrat Nithya Raman. The City Council member speaks with NPR's Ailsa Chang.
item.content:encoded: The votes have been counted, and the mayoral incumbent in Los Angeles -- Karen Bass -- will face fellow Democrat Nithya Raman. The City Council member speaks with NPR's Ailsa Chang.
item.dc:creator: Ailsa Chang

item.title: What is FISA's Section 702?
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Elizabeth Goitein of the Brennan Center for Justice, an advocate for reforming the controversial surveillance law known as FISA 702.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Elizabeth Goitein of the Brennan Center for Justice, an advocate for reforming the controversial surveillance law known as FISA 702.
item.dc:creator: Ailsa Chang

item.title: Getting a promotion at the Pentagon seems to be getting harder under Hegseth
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has blocked or delayed the promotions of several officers across the military branches, and a disproportionate number of those officers are women and people of color. Why? And how is Congress responding?
item.content:encoded: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has blocked or delayed the promotions of several officers across the military branches, and a disproportionate number of those officers are women and people of color. Why? And how is Congress responding?
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: World Cup 2026 kicks off in Mexico City
item.description: It's finally here! The World Cup 2026 kicks off in Mexico City -- and NPR is there.
item.content:encoded: It's finally here! The World Cup 2026 kicks off in Mexico City -- and NPR is there.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta

item.title: A decade after the Pulse nightclub massacre, grief is still raw in Orlando
item.description: It's been 10 years since the mass shooting at the Pulse nightclub in Orlando. Grief for the 49 young people who died is still felt throughout the community.
item.content:encoded: It's been 10 years since the mass shooting at the Pulse nightclub in Orlando. Grief for the 49 young people who died is still felt throughout the community.
item.dc:creator: Joe M Pedersen

item.title: A fuzzy little detective is helping police in Florida: Meet Splash the otter
item.description: Otters can be trained to do search and rescue in murky waters. In Florida, law enforcement is now using one of them.
item.content:encoded: Otters can be trained to do search and rescue in murky waters. In Florida, law enforcement is now using one of them.
item.dc:creator: Cathleen Carter
